course_description: Enrolment Note:This course has a delayed enrolment date and will open following the allocation of Honours streams. Students will be advised of the specific date each year when their Honours stream is confirmed and should contact the School of Psychology for further information. This course has an enrolment quota of 120 students per semester (across all modes) on a first-in basis. In this course, you will learn how to improve human performance using evidence-based applied cognitive psychology, both through training and system design. The topics will include expertise, automaticity, knowledge acquisition, how to practice, and skill transfer.

restrictions: BPsySc, BSc(Hons) in Psychology, BArts(Hons) in Psychology

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: My aim is to help you develop the skills to be able to improve human performance using evidence-based applied cognitive psychology, both via training and system design. By the end of this course, I want you to be workforce-ready.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain a range of approaches for improving human performance based on research from applied cognitive psychology.
Design and evaluate evidence-based training interventions.
Evaluate empirical research in applied cognitive psychology.
Have mastery over the material taught in the course.
Demonstrate the ability to work in a team.

assessment_summary.title: Second submission of assignment with cover letter
9 in-class quizzes
assessment_summary.weight: 40% Scores from all 9 quizzes will comprise the final quiz score
assessment_summary.due_date: 8/08/2025 12:00 pm; 15/08/2025 12:00 pm; 22/08/2025 12:00 pm; 29/08/2025 12:00 pm; 5/09/2025 12:00 pm; 12/09/2025 12:00 pm; 19/09/2025 12:00 pm; 17/10/2025 12:00 pm; 24/10/2025 12:00 pm; These quizzes will take place during workshops, and will be closed-book and invigilated. They cannot be completed outside of the workshops.

assessment_details.title: First submission of assignment
assessment_details.task_description: You will be writing a proposal for the design and evaluation of a training intervention in a domain of your own choosing. Full details will be provided in the Assignment Briefing. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The research proposal is to be submitted online via the PSYC4191 Blackboard website. Please check your submission receipt.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Video assignment
assessment_details.task_description: You will be assigned to groups of 4-5 students for the video assignment. The video assignment will involve you working in your group to make a brief (2-4 minute) video presentation on one of the topics covered by the course. These videos will be premiered in the final workshop and then released as part of a YouTube playlist after the course (unless you tell me otherwise). Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
One person per team needs to submit the team's video via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Contact the course co-ordinator directly for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Second submission of assignment with cover letter
assessment_details.task_description: You will be required to resubmit your research proposal having addressed all the feedback provided to you by the tutor. You will also have to submit a cover letter with your responses to all the points of feedback (as if you were responding to reviews of a journal article). See the Assignment Briefing (second submission) for more details. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.task_description: There will be 9 closed-book in-class quizzes during every workshop from Week 2 onwards. The average of your scores across all these quizzes will count towards your overall course score. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
If you miss a quiz for an appropriate reason and obtain a written exemption from the course co-ordinator, then you will be able to take a make-up quiz based on the same content. All make-up quizzes will be conducted in the Week 13 workshop ONLY. There are no opportunities for completing the quizzes outside of the workshops (see Quiz Briefing and Course Overview documents on Blackboard for further details).
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
